text: Working with APIs is a key skill for modern developers. When you make an HTTP request to an API, you typically receive data in JSON format, which is easy for programs to read. Many APIs require authentication, often using an API key, to ensure only authorised users can access the service. Before you start coding, it's important to check the API’s rate limits – these restrict how many requests you can make in a given time period. Finally, always include error handling in your code. Network issues or incorrect requests can happen, so your program should gracefully manage these situations and provide useful feedback to the user.
